name: Albaladejo and Montiel Castles Loop
name_original: Los Castillo de Albaladejo y Castillo de Montiel desde Almedina – circular por Villanueva de los Infantes y Torre de Juan Abad
description: This 45.5-mile (73.3 km) moderate road cycling route explores historical castles and charming towns in Spain's Campo de Montiel region.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Almedina traverse a rugged natural environment within the Campo de Montiel, characterized by the Guadalén River and its tributaries. The landscape features distinctive hills like El Bogallo and El Montón de Trigo, composed of red earth, alongside serene and defined plains. This varied terrain offers a mix of ascents and flatter sections, suitable for road cycling. Best road cycling routes around Almedina The most popular road cycling route is Los Castillo de Albaladejo y Castillo de Montiel desde Almedina – circular por Villanueva de los Infantes y Torre de Juan Abad, a 45.5 miles (73.3 km) trail that takes 3 hours 8 minutes to complete. This route offers a circular journey through the region's historical and natural landscapes. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Carrizosa from Villanueva de los Infantes – circular via Santa Cruz de los Cáñamos and Castillo de Montiel, a moderate 43.9 miles (70.7 km) path. This circular route explores the rural surroundings and passes by Castillo de Montiel. Local road cyclists also love the Castillo de Montiel from Albaladejo – circular via Torre de Juan Abad, a 43.8 miles (70.6 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 3 hours 0 minutes. Road cycling around Almedina is defined by its rugged natural environment, the Guadalén River, and distinctive hills. The network offers options for different ability levels, including moderate and difficult routes. The routes in Almedina are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 1 review. More than 30 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Almedina's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Almedina?
A: There are 7 road cycling routes available around Almedina, offering a variety of experiences for cyclists. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars.

Q: What is the typical terrain like for road cycling in Almedina?
A: The terrain around Almedina is rugged and varied, characterized by the Guadalén River and its tributaries, distinctive hills like El Bogallo and El Montón de Trigo composed of red earth, and serene plains. This combination offers a mix of ascents and flatter sections, providing diverse challenges and scenic views for road cyclists.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners or less experienced riders?
A: While the region is known for its rugged environment, the available routes are predominantly classified as moderate. There are no routes specifically designated as easy, so riders should be prepared for some climbs and varied terrain. The route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20129038" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montiel – Ermita del Cristo de Jamila loop from Montiel&lt;/a&gt; is one of the shorter moderate options, at about 27.8 miles (44.8 km).

Q: What about more challenging road cycling routes in the area?
A: There is one route classified as difficult among the available options, offering a more strenuous experience for seasoned road cyclists. The majority of routes are moderate, providing a good challenge with significant elevation changes, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1086164982" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torrenueva from Cózar – circular via Almedina and Torre de Juan Abad&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 56.8 miles (91.4 km) with over 550 meters of elevation gain.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes around Almedina?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes around Almedina are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1085974301/los-castillo-de-albaladejo-y-castillo-de-montiel-desde-almedina-circular-por-villanueva-de-los-infantes-y-torre-de-juan-abad" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Castillo de Albaladejo y Castillo de Montiel desde Almedina&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1086137267/carrizosa-from-villanueva-de-los-infantes-circular-via-santa-cruz-de-los-canamos-and-castillo-de-montiel" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Carrizosa from Villanueva de los Infantes&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What kind of landmarks or natural features can I expect to see on a road cycling trip near Almedina?
A: Road cycling routes near Almedina often pass through areas rich in natural beauty and historical sites. You can expect to see the rugged 'Los Barrancos' region, the Guadalén River, and distinctive red-earth hills. Historical landmarks include various castles like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5618247" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montiel Castle&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5903131" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santiago Apóstol and Albaladejo Castle&lt;/a&gt;, as well as religious buildings like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6633566" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita del Cristo de Jamila&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any family-friendly road cycling options in Almedina?
A: Given the moderate to difficult classification of the routes and the rugged terrain, dedicated family-friendly road cycling routes are not explicitly available. The region's routes are generally better suited for experienced road cyclists. For families, it might be advisable to explore other cycling options or shorter, less challenging sections if available.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Almedina?
A: While specific weather data isn't provided, the region of Ciudad Real, where Almedina is located, generally experiences hot summers. Spring and autumn are typically the most pleasant seasons for outdoor activities like road cycling, offering milder temperatures and more comfortable riding conditions. Winter can also be suitable, though temperatures will be cooler.

Q: Are there any specific viewpoints or scenic spots along the road cycling routes?
A: The routes traverse a naturally beautiful area, offering scenic views of the rugged 'Los Barrancos' region, the Guadalén River, and the unique red-earth hills. Many routes pass by historical sites such as castles, which often provide elevated perspectives of the surrounding landscape. For example, the route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1070331054/castillo-de-montiel-from-albaladejo-circular-via-torre-de-juan-abad" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Montiel from Albaladejo&lt;/a&gt; offers views of the castle and its surroundings.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy the most about road cycling in Almedina?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Reviewers often praise the rugged natural environment, the varied terrain that offers both climbs and flatter sections, and the opportunity to explore historical and natural landscapes away from heavy traffic.

Q: Are there any amenities like cafes or accommodation near the road cycling routes?
A: Almedina is a village in the heart of Campo de Montiel. While specific cafes or accommodations directly on the routes are not detailed, the routes often pass through or near other villages and towns in the region, such as Villanueva de los Infantes or Montiel, where you would typically find local amenities, cafes, and guesthouses. It's advisable to plan your route and check for services in these towns.

Q: Can I find parking for my car at the start of the road cycling routes?
A: While specific designated cycling parking is not detailed, as Almedina is a rural area, you can generally find parking in the villages and towns where these routes typically start or pass through. For example, routes starting from Almedina itself or nearby towns like Villanueva de los Infantes would have public parking options available.
tips: At one end of the town of Albaladejo is its castle which is together with the church of Santiago Apóstol. The walls are still there and can be visited!
From the lower part of the hill where the Castillo de Albaladejo is located, we can find good views of the town as a whole.

Montiel Castle, also known as the Star Castle, is one of the most important historical sites in Castile-La Mancha.
Built by the Arabs in the 9th century, under the name of Munt-Yil, it was part of the defense of the Islamic territory against the Christian advance.
It was conquered by the Christians in 1227, after a long siege, and passed into the hands of the Order of Santiago, who made it the center of the Commandery of Campo de Montiel.
The Tragedy of Montiel (1369) occurred here, where King Peter I of Castile was assassinated by his brother Henry of Trastámara, with Bertrand Duguesclin famously saying: "I neither remove nor appoint a king, but I help my lord."
It is currently in a state of partial ruin, but excavations and guided tours are taking place.

The Plaza Mayor of Villanueva de los Infantes is considered one of the most beautiful main squares in Castilla-La Mancha, not in vain has it been declared a Historic-Artistic Site, with notable buildings such as the Town Hall, emblazoned mansions, porticoed gallery, the Hospital de Santiago, the Convent of Santo Domingo or the Church of San Andrés Apóstol.

The Parish Church of Our Lady of the Elms, in Torre de Juan Abad (Ciudad Real), is one of the most emblematic churches in Campo de Montiel, with great historical and artistic value.
It was built on the site of a small existing chapel in 1243, a work of the Order of Santiago, whose cross-sword can still be seen in the pediments.
Declared a Site of Cultural Interest (BIC) in 2005.

faqs.question: How long does this road cycling route typically take?
faqs.answer: This road cycling route is approximately 73.3 kilometers long and typically takes around 3 hours and 8 minutes to complete, depending on your pace and stops.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle this route is generally during spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October) when the weather in Ciudad Real, Spain, is milder and more pleasant for road cycling. Summers can be very hot, and winters can be chilly.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route is classified as a 'Moderate road ride,' meaning you can expect mostly well-paved surfaces that are generally easy to ride. The elevation gain of 570 meters suggests a rolling or gently hilly terrain rather than consistently flat or strenuously mountainous sections.

faqs.question: What historical sites and landmarks can I see along the way?
faqs.answer: This route is rich in history and offers several notable features. You'll pass by the Santiago Apóstol and Albaladejo Castle, enjoy a View of Albaladejo Castle, and see the historic Montiel Castle. Other highlights include the Plaza Mayor de Villanueva de los Infantes and the Church of Our Lady of the Elms in Torre de Juan Abad.

faqs.question: Is this road cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Given its 'moderate' difficulty rating, 73.3 km distance, and 570 meters of elevation gain, this route is best suited for cyclists with a good level of fitness and some experience with road cycling. It might be challenging for absolute beginners.

faqs.question: Where can I park my car to start this cycling route?
faqs.answer: While the route is circular and can be joined at various points, Almedina is a common starting point. You can look for parking options in Almedina or other towns along the route such as Villanueva de los Infantes, Albaladejo, Montiel, or Torre de Juan Abad.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle this public road route. However, individual attractions or historical sites you choose to visit along the way may have their own admission fees.

faqs.question: Are there cafes or places to get refreshments along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route passes through several towns including Almedina, Villanueva de los Infantes, Albaladejo, Montiel, and Torre de Juan Abad. These towns offer opportunities to find cafes, pubs, and other establishments for refreshments and meals.

faqs.question: Is this road cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: As this is a road cycling route, it is not typically considered 'dog-friendly' in the same way a hiking trail might be. While you may pass through towns where dogs are allowed on leashes, cycling with a dog on a road bike for this distance and terrain is generally not recommended for safety reasons.

faqs.question: Does the route offer any scenic viewpoints?
faqs.answer: Yes, near Almedina, there is a viewpoint offering panoramic vistas of the town, surrounded by characteristic olive groves and agricultural land, with the tower of Santa María Parish Church visible in the distance. The route also offers picturesque views of the Campo de Montiel region.

faqs.question: Are there public transport options to reach the starting points of this route?
faqs.answer: Public transport options to reach towns like Almedina, Villanueva de los Infantes, or Torre de Juan Abad may be limited, especially for cyclists with bikes. It's advisable to check local bus schedules or consider driving to a suitable starting point with parking.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect while cycling?
faqs.answer: You can expect a blend of historical and natural beauty. The route takes you through landscapes characterized by olive groves and farmland, particularly around Almedina, and connects significant historical sites within the Campo de Montiel region.
